title: Box Office Projection: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple Eyes $20M+ MLK Opening
text: Sony's horror sequel is projected to earn $20M+ over the MLK holiday weekend (4-day total), dethroning Avatar: Fire and Ash from the number one position in its fifth week. Avatar: Fire and Ash is expected to earn approximately $18M-$19M during the same period, despite retaining most IMAX and premium large format screens. The film opens at 3,400 North American theaters, with preview screenings beginning Thursday at 2 p.m. across 2,900 locations. Primary tracking indicates strongest interest among men over 25, with heat levels comparable to 2023's Evil Dead Rise ($24.5M opening) and 2024's Nosferatu ($21.6M three-day). Director Nia DaCosta's entry in the franchise follows the previous installment's strong performance. Last summer's 28 Years Later opened to $30M domestically and grossed $70.4M stateside, $151.3M globally. The production budget for this sequel was $63M net. The MLK weekend traditionally marks the conclusion of holiday moviegoing, with limited counterprogramming available this year.
